steps.title: Translate the percentage increase to a growth factor
steps.content:
Increases by 0.49% of its value means the new value is the old value plus 0.49% of the old value:
New value = Old value + 0.0049 × Old value = Old value × (1 + 0.0049) = Old value × 1.0049

steps.title: Recognize the exponential pattern
steps.content:
Each year, we multiply the previous value by 1.0049:
Year 1: $V_0 \times 1.0049$
Year 2: $V_0 \times 1.0049^2$
Year $t$: $$V(t) = V_0(1.0049)^t$$
The variable $t$ is in the exponent, making this exponential (not linear).

steps.title: Determine if increasing or decreasing
steps.content: The base of the exponential is 1.0049, which is greater than 1. Multiplying by a number greater than 1 repeatedly produces larger values. This is an increasing exponential function. That matches choice C — C is correct.
